characters.name: "Weird Al" Yankovic
characters.description: "Weird Al" YankovicAlso Known AsAl Yankovic (Real name)OccupationTV Star, Singer and Song WriterFriendsBlue Falcon, Don Knotts, Johnny BravoFirst AppearanceJohnny MakeoverVoiced By"Weird Al" Yankovic "Weird Al" Yankovicis a guest star character who appeared in the episode "Johnny Makeover". He is a fictional version of the real-life singer and song writer of the same name who is famously known for his song parodies and polka covers of popular songs. "Weird Al" along with theBlue FalconandDon Knottsare the stars of a TV show called "Cartoon Makeover", which is known for the three stars making improvements to cartoons, particularly Hanna-Barbera cartoons, and they wanted to make improvements to Johnny's show itself. This version of "Weird Al" is noticeably rather self-absorbed and insensitive compared to the real-life "Weird Al" who appears as a more nicer guy in general, and he's arguably the most critical of Johnny's show out of the three stars.
characters.personality_section: This version of "Weird Al" is noticeably rather self-absorbed and insensitive compared to the real-life "Weird Al" who appears as a more nicer guy in general, and he's arguably the most critical of Johnny's show out of the three stars.

characters.name: Adam West
characters.description: Adam Westis a guest star character inJohnny Bravo. He made his first appearance in the episode "Johnny Bravo Meets Adam West!", and his second in "Adam West's Date-O-Rama", where he was the host of a dating show. He was voiced byAdam West himself.

characters.name: Adam West
characters.description: Adam Westis a guest star character inJohnny Bravo. He made his first appearance in the episode "Johnny Bravo Meets Adam West!", and his second in "Adam West's Date-O-Rama", where he was the host of a dating show. He was voiced byAdam West himself.

characters.name: Becky the Moose
characters.description: Becky the MooseAlso Known AsBecky Jones(undercover identity)OccupationBar waitress(formerly)RelativesJohnny Bravo (undercover husband)FriendsJohnnyVoiced ByMelissa Peterman Becky the Mooseis an anthropomorphic moose that appeared in "Wilderness Protection Program". She was a witness to what she believed was a secret conversation between animal mobsters. To ensure her safety, she must live undercover withJohnny Bravo, posing as a married elephant couple. —Becky Becky is energetic, loud, and easily emotional. Due to her size, she tends to be accident-prone. She takes her undercover role more seriously than Johnny, acting as the typical housewife. She is also notably just as air-headed and dimwitted as Johnny himself, even wasting hours trying to figure out a simple clue and even believing Johnny's completely outrageous claim about the mafia planning to steal honey from the bears in order to help the the mole-men conquer the world.

characters.name: Big Brother
characters.description: Big BrotherAgeUnknownOccupationSupreme Dictator, Comedian and Television HostFirst AppearanceBrave New JohnnyVoiced ByTim Curry —Big Brother Big Brotheris the supreme tyrannical dictator of the 25th century, having enslaved much of the world and the loyalty of the masses through his daily and mandatory self-hosted variety TV showThe Big Brother Comedy Hour, which provides his subjects with mind-numbing entertainment and stand-up comedy courtesy of Big Brother himself which are in actuality subliminal reminders of the consequences that come to those who question Big Brother's rule. Big Brother initially appears as a charming comedian who maintains an air of warmth and good humor like a typical television host to mask his evil nature. In truth, he is a tyrannical despot who uses entertainment to control the masses and enforce laws that limit food, basic necessities and even romance, with even kissing being forbidden under his authority and the citizens being forced to eat moldy damp soy mulch. Under his rule, the future lacks many of the necessities Johnny had expected, with regular gasoline-power cars still in use, no robots anywhere to be seen, cockroach mutants living alongside humans, and a highly polluted ecosystem which has caused the world to appear dark and dismal with red skies and an orange hue permeating everywhere. He commands armies of loyal followers that enforce his will and sends those who question his will to re-education camps. His ban on kissing has also caused normal romantic interaction to be replaced with "Hyper-Lip Mandibulator", which instead cause the recipient to experience horribly painful yet enticing electric shocks with each transmitted kissing sensation. Big Brother also seems to take some genuine pride in his status as the world's sole entertainer and comedian, being greatly offended when Johnny heckled his act and said his show stunk, with Big Brother even admitting that he has all of his critics seized.

characters.name: Blue Falcon
characters.description: Blue FalconAlso Known AsRadley Crown (Secret Identity)OccupationTV Star, Super heroFriends"Weird Al" Yankovic, Don Knotts, Johnny BravoFirst Appearance"Johnny Makeover"Voiced ByGary Owens The Blue Falconis a super hero who served as the co-star for the Hanna-Barbera cartoon seriesDynomutt, Dog Wonder. He is the owner of Dynomutt the Dog Wonder, to which they both work as a super hero duo that fight crime in Big City. The Blue Falcon along with"Weird Al" YankovicandDon Knottsare the stars of a TV show called "Cartoon Makeover", which features the trio attempting to make changes to TV shows particularly Hanna-Barbera cartoons in order for them to be more suitable to target audiences. And they attempted to the same for Johnny's show in "Johnny Makeover".

And they attempted to the same for Johnny's show in "Johnny Makeover".

characters.name: Bobo
characters.description: BoboOccupationCook at Pop's Moon PalaceFriendsPops, Johnny, CarlFirst Appearance"Bikini Space Planet!"Last Appearance"Scoop Bravo"Voiced ByFrank Welker Bobois a minor character who is the head cook atPops' Moon Palace, as well as the only employee who has worked there. He had small roles throughout Seasons 2 and 3, having fewer appearances as time went on. Bobo wears a green mask, a white tank top, an apron, red striped pants, and a red bowtie. Bobo doesn't speak many words, instead usually making grunting and humming sounds. He doesn't express much bravery as he seemed to be easily afraid ofPopsin "A Gel For Johnny".

characters.name: Bunny Bravo
characters.description: Bunny BravoAlso Known AsMama BravoMrs. BravoMadame X(Alias in "Johnny's Guardian Angel")Mother Hen(Codename in "A Walk On The Stupid Side")AgeUnknown (Seasons 1 & 4)60 (Seasons 2-3)[3]Height5'5"OccupationJohnny's MotherDen Mother("Den Mother Johnny")Girl's Softball Coach("A League Of His Own")Racecar Driver(formerly)RelativesJohnny Bravo(Son)Jebedissa Bravo(Sister)Myra Bravo(Sister, mentioned)[1]Shirley Bravo(Sister, mentioned)Lou Bravo(Brother, deceased)Fiona Bravo(Aunt, mentioned)[2]FriendsJohnny Bravo,Little Suzy,Donny Osmond, French Teacher, Carl Chryniszzswics, and variousFirst Appearance"Bearly Enough Time"Last Appearance"Johnny Bravo Goes to Bollywood"Voiced ByBrenda Vaccaro Bunny "Mama" BravoisJohnny Bravo's mother. She's had a lively past and often reveals a surprising hidden talent. Bunny's an eccentric but nurturing woman who's very loving and dedicated to her son -- despite that, Bunny is generally aware of her son's low intellect (as well as his womanizing nature). This has the unfortunate result of Bunny not always treating her son and/or his problems seriously. Bunny is kind, outgoing, eccentric and loving, often babying Johnny (even though he's technically an adult). She is usually nice, but she will give a beating to anyone who thinks they can seriously harm her son or treat him unfairly with her around. Bunny is a huge fan of shopping networks, knickknacks (such as collectible plates, porcelain figurines, and dolls), and a soap opera called "The Young and the Feckless", a spoof onThe Young and the Restless. She is also a huge fan of certain celebrities, such as the singerBerry Vanderbolten. Bunny has a passion for gardening and some of her other hobbies include cooking (such as baking), sports (even extreme sports) and playing bingo. Bunny's been shown to not be the greatest cook ever (although she appears to be talented at making desserts and baked goods), but Johnny's so used to his mom's cooking that he'll eat basically anything she makes. In one comic story, Johnny (much to the disgust of his nerdy friend, Carl) claims "everything Mama cooks tastes like chicken."[4]

He helped reviveJohnnyafter he had been encased in hair gel until the year 2499.

characters.name: Carl Chryniszzswics
characters.description: Carl ChryniszzswicsAlso Known AsWeed(by Johnny)Weasel Boy(by Johnny)Skinny(by Helga)Chow Dog(codename in "A Walk On The Stupid Side")AgeEarly 20s[1]OccupationInventor/ScientistComputer Camp Counselor("Johnny Goes To Camp")Weenie's On A Rod Assistant Manager Trainee("Law And Disorder")RelativesUnnamed ParentsCarl #97324(Great-Great-Great-Great-Grandson)Unnamed Deputy (Ancestor)("Lone Star Bravo")Kharlok(Possible, "Galaxy Boy")FriendsJohnny, Pops, Bunny Bravo, Little SuzyFirst Appearance"Bikini Space Planet!"Last Appearance"Johnny Makeover"(non-speaking cameo appearance)Voiced ByTom Kenny(USA)Janusz Zadura (14-26) and Józef Mika (27-62) (Poland) —Carl, seconds before Johnny does exactly that Carl ChryniszzswicsisJohnny Bravo's neighbor who debuted inSeason 2. Carl is Johnny's best friend, a local genius, and a geek. He is often considered annoying by others, mostly due to his high-pitched nasal voice. Carl is intelligent, inventive, eccentric, timid, and friendly. He is often over-enthusiastic, talking a lot and clapping his hands or exclaiming words such as "Yay!" and "Okay!" when excited. His main interests are science, sci-fi (especiallyTrip McCoy: Galaxy Boy), and bugs. Carl's other interests include history, mythology, and various cultures. He also knows a lot about nature, such as plants, wildlife, recycling, etc. Carl is a huge fan of role-playing. In "Carl Be Not Proud", Carl makes Johnny play the role of Biff Proton while he plays Trip McCoy, dressing in full costume. In "Law And Disorder", Carl stalls for time by suggesting a therapeutic role-playing session between he and the mall burglar where he poorly sings as cabaret singer Billie Holiday. While part of the town fire brigade, Carl claims the activity is his favorite part of being a fireman, shortly before playing the role of a cat in a tree and frightening both Johnny andPopsin the process.[2] Carl seems to have a strong sense of justice. In "Hail To The Chump", after Johnny's new law as mayor lands him in prison, Carl tries to rally the townspeople together to end Johnny's rule. In "Lord Of The Links", Carl helps Johnny cheat in a golf tournament and reveals that one competitor was cheating via magnets and iron shavings. Due to their cheating being just as unfair, Carl gives the trophy toThe Prospector, getting beaten by the angry losers as a result. Carl's social skills can vary, as he can also be impulsive, forceful, or misunderstand a situation (i.e., "I didn't know you liked her!"). Carl's nowhere near as girl-crazy as Johnny, but he has been shown to have crushes at various points in the series -- also, despite not being as girl-crazy and his wimpier/nerdier appearance, Carl tends to be more successful with women than Johnny is, which (of course) irritates the meathead.

characters.name: Carol the Antelope
characters.description: Carol(voiced byGabrielle Carteris) was a pronghorn who datedJohnnyin "Date With An Antelope". The two meet online, however Johnny was unaware that Carol was an antelope. When they meet in person, Carol starts to cry after she realizes that Johnny is not interested. He humbly told her that there was not any problem and went on a pity date with her. They went to a carnival and to a restaurant, and even though Carol seemed to like Johnny, she dated him only because she wanted to teach her jealous boyfriend (a crab named Ned) a lesson. Her boyfriend ended up fighting with Johnny and both of them went to jail.

Her boyfriend ended up fighting with Johnny and both of them went to jail.

characters.name: CD Biggenz
characters.description: CD Biggenz is a hip hop singer who's part of the Round Pound group, voiced by Chuck D.

characters.name: CD Biggenz
characters.description: CD Biggenz is a hip hop singer who's part of the Round Pound group, voiced by Chuck D.

characters.name: Chronos
characters.description: ChronosAgeUnknownOccupationMaster of Time(supposedly)First AppearanceBearly Enough TimeVoiced ByBrock Peters Chronosis a very largegrizzly bearwho lives in the woods nearAron Citywho claims to be the "Master of All Time" whose slumber was disturbed byJohnnyin the episode"Bearly Enough Time." When Johnny stumbles into this cave, he trips and breaks every clock there, angering Chronos to the point of wanting to eat Johnny. Before doing so though, he tried to impress Johnny with his so-called "mastery" over time with rather mundane feats, which gave Johnny the chance to convince Chronos that he could put him back to sleep. After several failed attempts, Johnny was able to outwit Chronos and escape his cave, but Chronos remained in pursuit. Luckily they ran intoBunny Bravowho was singing a calming lullaby which lulled Chronos and Johnny to sleep. Unfortunately when Johnny woke up, he found himself back in his own bed but with a sleeping Chronos tucked in with him by Bunny, making Johnny realize he might be stuck with Chronos for the next six months. Chronos calls himself the master of time and lives in a cave filled with various sorts of clocks, though he cannot change the time on his VCR. Whether he really is a Master of Time or not is debatable since aside from summoning lightning when he yells out his name, his influence over time doesn't seem to extend beyond keeping all of his clocks in order, maintaining a strict schedule and correcting pre-recorded phone messages when they give the wrong time, suggesting that he may just be deluded.

characters.name: Daphne Blake
characters.description: Daphne Blakeis a member of the Mystery Inc. Gang, a group of amateur teenage detectives who travel around the country in their van  solving mysteries with their talking dog Scooby-Doo. She is one of the main characters from theScooby-Doo!franchise who made a guest appearance in"Bravo Dooby-Doo", serving as the main target forJohnny's flirtatious antics throughout the episode. Daphne has been a part of the Mystery Inc. Gang since childhood and they have been traveling and solving mysteries ever since, going on all sorts of spooky misadventures. The Mystery Inc. Gang picked up a hitchhikingJohnnywho was on his way to spend time with his auntJebedissa Bravo. Once introduced, Johnny wasted no time in eyeing Daphne and making his move, only to be soundly rejected every time. When they arrive at Jebedissa's manor, they find the place is now haunted by theGhostly Gardenerwho has seemingly kidnapped aunt Jebedissa, putting the Mystery Gang into action to search for clues. Johnny initially tries to team up with Daphne when they split up but is soundly rejected, and Johnny's hopes are further crushed when Daphen decides to initially go with Shaggy, only to be approached by Fred who seductively reminded that they always "split up" together. After several hijinks, the gang eventually captures the ghost and discover that it was Aunt Jebedissa all along who confesses that she was just trying to get rid of the annoying Johnny. The regretful gang apologize and ask how they can make it up to her, and she forces them to dump Johnny off in the woods and let her tag alone instead, which is soon followed by her getting awkwardly close to the gang.

characters.name: Derek
characters.description: DerekAlso Known AsScion of DarknessOccupationSpreading evilEnemiesAll things good, Johnny,Pops,CarlVoiced ByTom Kenny Derekis a demon from the underworld who was summoned byJohnnyin the episode "Johnny's Inferno". He tries to trick Johnny into committing (relatively mundane) acts of evil, but fails numerous times due to coincidences that end up benefitting others. Derek is a short imp or chort-like demon with typical cloven hooves, pointy tail, sharp claws, sharp teeth and dark red hair who wears a simple red-colored business jacket with a white dress shirt complete with a little black necktie. Unlike most demons, Derek does not have any horns, as he explains that he has not yet earned them due to having no luck tempting sinners into doing evil deeds. Derek declares himself to be a demonic being of pure evil dedicated to spreading chaos and misery wherever he goes, however his idea of what qualifies as "evil" is rather pathetic to say the least, as most of his evil deeds consist of simply annoying people or causing mild inconveniences, like paying in pennies or cutting in lines. He also prefers not to perform evil deeds himself, since according to Derek's own words, an evil deed is only truly evil if a mortal (ie Johnny) does it.

characters.name: Doctor Fathead
characters.description: Doctor Fathead in woman disguise. Doctor Fatheadis a doctor that appears in "As I Lay Hiccupping". He appears toJohnnyas a young, thin woman with long brown hair and pink lipstick. However, he is actually a man who wears a disguise as a "scare cure for hiccups". In his actual form, he is bald, hairy, and overweight. Johnny flirting with the doctor.

characters.name: Don Knotts
characters.description: Don KnottsOccupationTV star, comedian, actorFriends"Weird Al" Yankovic, Blue Falcon, Jessica Biel, Hobbit, Alec Baldwin, Johnny BravoFirst Appearance"Bravo Dooby-Doo" (Cameo)Last Appearance"Johnny Makeover"Voiced ByDon Knotts Don Knottsis a recurring guest star character inJohnny Bravo. He is a fictional version of the real-life actor and comedian of the same name who is known for starring in many comedic films such asThe Ghost and Mr. Chicken, and is also known for his role as Deputy Barney Fife fromThe Andy Griffith Show. He first made a cameo appearance in "Bravo Dooby-Doo" as one ofJebedissa Bravo'sred herring disguises. He made a much more bigger appearance in "Johnny Bravo Goes to Hollywood" where he was one of the stars of the movie that Johnny was originally cast in known as "Lunch Lady S'urprise." His final appearance was in "Johnny Makeover", where he along with"Weird Al" Yankovicand theBlue Falconwere the stars of a TV show called "Cartoon Makeover", where they served as a trio who would try to make changes to TV shows particularly Hanna-Barbera cartoons in order for them to be more suitable to target audiences. And from watching Johnny's show, they attempted to do the same to his.

characters.name: Donny Osmond
characters.description: Donny OsmondAlso Known AsJim Dandy (series bible)OccupationNanny, Singer, Dancer, Television Host, ActorFriendsJohnny Bravo, Little Suzy, Bunny Bravo, Leo the Pilot, Santa ClausFirst Appearance"Johnny Meets Donny Osmond"Last Appearance"That's Entertainment!"Voiced ByHimself Donny Osmondis a recurring guest star character inJohnny Bravo. He is based on the real-life pop singer of the same name. Donny first appears in theSeason 1episode "Johnny Meets Donny Osmond" where he wasJohnny'snanny, he made his second appearance in "A Johnny Bravo Christmas", his third appearance in "It's Valentines Day Johnny Bravo", a cameo appearance in "Home Alone", and his final appearance in theSeason 4episode "That's Entertainment!" Donny is a very friendly and peppy guy who has happy-go-lucky interactions with other characters, including Johnny. While he never seems to get angry or sad, he has been shown to take offense, but can get over it quickly through looking on the bright side. Like when Johnny threw his guitar away over a mountain, which caused Donny to feel unhappy, but he got over it quickly since he also brought his bagpipes with him.

characters.name: Dr. Alphonse
characters.description: Dr. Alphonseis a male nurse, who made his first and only appearance in the episodeIntensive Care. He made his first appearance when Johnny (who came dress like a clown for Suzi) encountered a pretty nurse, who he tried to hit on and pretended to be sick, However Alphonse appeared with her and was asked to take care of him. Throughout his appearance in the episode, Alphonse was shown to cause more harm them care towards Johnny, such as knocking him out the window with hospital bed, dropping a piano on him, so he rides a wheelchair, where he fell downstairs, using the automatic window to hurt him, followed by using the hospital bed to make him into a sandwich, using a dog whistle to summon a lot of dogs and finally, removing his liver. To make matters worse, When Johnny contracted Chicken-Measles from Little Suzi, Alphonse would appear and take care of Johnny, when he had an itch, which he plans to use rubbing alcohol and sandpaper. While being viewed as a creepy man with a creeper mannerism, he is actually shown to be a good person, due to helping nurse Amber dealing with Johnny, who pretended to be sick in order to hit on her, but he takes his role as a male nurse too seriously as he started to hurt Johnny rather than taking care of him, to the point where he removed Johnny's liver. While being viewed as a creepy man with a creeper mannerism, he is actually shown to be a good person, due to helping nurse Amber dealing with Johnny, who pretended to be sick in order to hit on her, but he takes his role as a male nurse too seriously as he started to hurt Johnny rather than taking care of him, to the point where he removed Johnny's liver.

characters.name: Dr. Rachel Levy
characters.description: Dr. Rachel LevyAlso Known AsExtraterrestrial MommaOccupationDentist, ScientistFriendsCarl,Dr. Fritz,Dr. WangEnemiesJohnnyFirst Appearance"Biosphere Johnny"Voiced ByAudrey Wasilewski —Dr. Rachel Levy, seconds before Johnny ruined everything Dr. Rachel Levyis (according toCarl) the world's foremost and most renowned "dental hygienist" (aka a dentist) who aspired to become a biologist and create the perfect artificial biosphere for human habitation with other doctors beforeJohnnycame along and threw a wrench into her plans. She appeared in the episode"Biosphere Johnny". Dr. Levy is very dedicated to her goals and will do whatever it takes to make sure her biosphere project succeeds, even being willing to eat one of her fellow crewmates (mostly just Johnny) just to ensure the project's survival. Like most women Johnny encounters, she hated his guts and subjected him to physical pain whenever he tried flirting, and she became even more hostile after Johnny ruined the balance of her artificial biosphere's ecosystems.

She is the one of the main characters of the episode "In Your Dreams". Dream Girl is essentially everything Johnny has ever wanted in a woman: gorgeous, flirty, and she smells like bologna. characters.name: Estron-3
characters.description: The Estronians of Estron-3. Estron-3(the most pleasant-smelling asteroid in the Delta Galaxy) is the home planet of the Estronians, an all-female race of humanoid space aliens with shiny/metallic-looking skin and unusually-colored hairstyles for each member of the species. Their only appearance was in "Bikini Space Planet!" (the first episode of Season 2), where they meetJohnny BravoatPop's Moon Palaceand take him back to their home planet. As they explain to him, the Estronians have searched the galaxy looking for "the perfect male specimen" to serve as their king and satisfy their dating needs.

characters.name: Female Johnny Bravo
characters.description: Female Johnny BravoAgeMid 20sFriendsJohnny, her kids, CarlFirst AppearanceI Dream Of JohnnyVoiced ByJeff Bennett —Female Johnny TheFemale Johnny Bravowas a magically manifested being created byJasperinethe genie to serve as Johnny's ideal wife, however she was too much like Johnny himself for his own liking. Her only appearance was in"I Dream Of Johnny". The Female Johnny is just as thickheaded and dimwitted as the real Johnny, if not more so to the point where even the real Johnny found her insufferable. Much like Johnny she loves showing off and talking about herself, even spouting nonsensical personal trivia about herself like Johnny. Like Johnny, she will also get verbally confused even to more extremes than Johnny, even briefly confusing the word "kids" with "mangoes".

characters.name: Femme
characters.description: FemmeAlso Known AsThe Blonde Lady from the Park(by Johnny)OccupationFemme FataleToy Market SwindlerEnemiesJohnny (formerly)Voiced ByGrey DeLisle Toy Market Swindler Femmeis a minor character inJohnny Bravo. She is the antagonist of the episode "Noir Johnny". As her name would suggest, Femme is a typical femme fatale criminal whoJohnnyinterrogated aboutSuzy'smissing doll. She refuses to tell him anything she might know about the case, and is later caught with it red-handed at the toy store that evening. Johnny tries to stop her and learns of her plan to hoard the dolls and sell them to children at "jacked-up" prices. Planning to escape, Femme runs over Johnny in a tiny toy car before Suzy arrives at the scene with policemen. Outside, Femme tries to convince Johnny to "let her off the hook", which he refuses. She then reveals that she had fallen in love with him from the start. After giving him a kiss on the cheek, she is taken away by police and never seen again. Femme often uses terms from hard-boiled detective fiction, such as "gumshoe", "snoop", and "stool pigeon". Her character was designed byDexter Smith.

Planning to escape, Femme runs over Johnny in a tiny toy car before Suzy arrives at the scene with policemen. Outside, Femme tries to convince Johnny to "let her off the hook", which he refuses. She then reveals that she had fallen in love with him from the start. After giving him a kiss on the cheek, she is taken away by police and never seen again. Femme often uses terms from hard-boiled detective fiction, such as "gumshoe", "snoop", and "stool pigeon". Her character was designed byDexter Smith.
characters.personality_section: As her name would suggest, Femme is a typical femme fatale criminal whoJohnnyinterrogated aboutSuzy'smissing doll. She refuses to tell him anything she might know about the case, and is later caught with it red-handed at the toy store that evening. Johnny tries to stop her and learns of her plan to hoard the dolls and sell them to children at "jacked-up" prices. Planning to escape, Femme runs over Johnny in a tiny toy car before Suzy arrives at the scene with policemen. Outside, Femme tries to convince Johnny to "let her off the hook", which he refuses. She then reveals that she had fallen in love with him from the start. After giving him a kiss on the cheek, she is taken away by police and never seen again. Femme often uses terms from hard-boiled detective fiction, such as "gumshoe", "snoop", and "stool pigeon". Her character was designed byDexter Smith.

characters.name: Fish Lips Malone
characters.description: Fish Lips Maloneis a dangerous gangster who appears in the episode "Some Like It Stupid". Fish Lips Malone is shown to be bald short middle age dark skin man with big lips (hench the nickname Fish lips) and his attire included a stereotypical 1920's gangster suit. He made his first appearance in a phone booth where upon learning he need to put in 75 cents more, he accidentally broke the booth and was given a large number of coins. He looked around hoping no one saw him as he said that if he gets arrested one more time, he will be locked up for good. However, Johnny witnesses his actions and Fish Lips plans to "Gut him like a Kipper", only to be distracted with a sale on Ice Picks.
